multiple_choice.question: In Jason's story, what turns a moment of shared amusement into a moral failure?
options: A. The class laughs at Joey before Jason says anything.
B. Jason and Joey mock the artwork without realizing the artist is present, and the joking goes too far.
C. Mr. Rogg invites the class to criticize the drawing.
D. The girl leaves the room before anyone reacts.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. Jason and Joey mock the artwork without realizing the artist is present, and the joking goes too far.

multiple_choice.question: When Dad initially replies, "That's not a crime," he is temporarily treating Jason's reaction as
options: A. a matter of taste rather than behavior.
B. a legal issue rather than a classroom issue.
C. an act of dishonesty rather than mockery.
D. a failure of responsibility rather than a private opinion.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: A. a matter of taste rather than behavior.

multiple_choice.question: Which expression from the Spotlight conversation most clearly signals that a further and more damaging detail is still coming?
options: A. "So what?"
B. "I couldn't help myself."
C. "That's not the worst of it."
D. "You're probably right."
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. "That's not the worst of it."

multiple_choice.question: In context, Jason's statement "I could just kick myself" conveys
options: A. anger at Mr. Rogg for leaving the room.
B. a desire to punish Joey physically.
C. intense self-reproach.
D. fear of being suspended.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. intense self-reproach.

multiple_choice.question: Dad's advice is structured so that, after apologizing and owning up, Jason should additionally
options: A. explain that Joey started the whole incident.
B. wait for the girl to speak first.
C. talk to Mr. Rogg afterward so he knows Jason did the right thing.
D. ask the class to apologize together.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. talk to Mr. Rogg afterward so he knows Jason did the right thing.

multiple_choice.question: Which general conclusion is most strongly supported by the article "Telling the Truth? It's Not So Easy!"?
options: A. Lying is statistically ordinary rather than exceptional in daily life.
B. Most lies are told only in professional settings.
C. Participants lied mainly to strangers rather than to familiar people.
D. White lies are presented as more harmful than direct insults.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: A. Lying is statistically ordinary rather than exceptional in daily life.

multiple_choice.question: Why does the article single out store clerks, salespeople, politicians, and journalists?
options: A. They were the only professions represented in the study.
B. They are treated as morally worse than other workers.
C. They illustrate professions in which heavy social contact creates more opportunities to lie.
D. They are the only people who tell white lies.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. They illustrate professions in which heavy social contact creates more opportunities to lie.

multiple_choice.question: Which claim would the article most clearly reject?
options: A. People sometimes lie to protect another person's feelings.
B. Some people are rewarded for lying in everyday situations.
C. Ordinary life includes many days on which no one tells even a single lie.
D. Frequent social interaction can increase opportunities for dishonesty.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. Ordinary life includes many days on which no one tells even a single lie.

multiple_choice.question: Which clause on page 16 exemplifies possession rather than time or place?
options: A. where people are completely honest all the time
B. when some form of lying wasn't a part of everyday life
C. whose jobs require frequent social contact
D. with whom they interacted
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. whose jobs require frequent social contact

multiple_choice.question: According to the caution box on page 17, which option is correct in formal English?
options: A. the people with who they interacted
B. the question to that most people don't give a truthful answer
C. the people who they interacted with
D. the people with whom they interacted
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: D. the people with whom they interacted

multiple_choice.question: Which single change converts the formal pattern on page 17 into the informal one without changing the core meaning?
options: A. Move the preposition to the end of the clause.
B. Replace whom with whose.
C. Delete the relative pronoun entirely.
D. Add commas around the clause.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: A. Move the preposition to the end of the clause.

multiple_choice.question: In "The workplace is the place where people tend to tell the most lies," the clause introduced by "where" functions as
options: A. a comment clause about the whole sentence.
B. a possession clause.
C. an identifying location clause.
D. an object clause after a preposition.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. an identifying location clause.

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence contains a relative pronoun that must directly follow a preposition?
options: A. The people who break their promises cannot be trusted.
B. The deal, about which Nora talked all the time, fell through.
C. There are moments when being honest can cause problems.
D. People whose lies are discovered lose trust.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. The deal, about which Nora talked all the time, fell through.

multiple_choice.question: Why is "which" correct in "He broke his sister's camera, which made him feel terrible"?
options: A. It identifies which camera he broke.
B. It replaces the noun "sister."
C. It comments on the entire independent clause.
D. It marks possession.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. It comments on the entire independent clause.

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence from the unit best illustrates a non-restrictive clause about a person?
options: A. The friend whose phone I lost insisted I didn't need to replace it.
B. Lara, who works in my office, told me she broke the camera.
C. The hotel in the town where we stayed last weekend offered to give us a refund.
D. The person who borrowed my camera yesterday just told me she had broken it.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. Lara, who works in my office, told me she broke the camera.

multiple_choice.question: The contrast between "The laptop which I bought last week is great" and "The laptop, which I bought last week, is great" is primarily a contrast between
options: A. certainty and doubt.
B. essential identification and additional comment.
C. formal and informal register.
D. active and passive structure.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. essential identification and additional comment.

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence would violate the unit's rule for comment clauses?
options: A. She blamed Paul for causing the accident, which was totally unfair.
B. I offered to pay for dinner, which was the right thing to do.
C. He broke his sister's camera, that made him feel terrible.
D. I had avoided taking responsibility, which was embarrassing.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. He broke his sister's camera, that made him feel terrible.

multiple_choice.question: In the tablet dialogue, which line most clearly shows acceptance of responsibility rather than emotion alone?
options: A. "I feel awful about it."
B. "They said it wouldn't be worth it."
C. "I'm going to get you a new one."
D. "Please accept my apology."
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. "I'm going to get you a new one."

multiple_choice.question: Tim's response "That's really not necessary" functions mainly to
options: A. deny that the damage happened.
B. soften the exchange by reducing the practical burden.
C. suggest the speaker should do nothing at all.
D. imply that the store can still repair the tablet.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. soften the exchange by reducing the practical burden.

multiple_choice.question: Which recycled expression on page 19 most strongly suggests failure to control a situation rather than direct dishonesty?
options: A. tell a lie
B. make up an excuse
C. let things get out of hand
D. shift the blame to someone else
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. let things get out of hand

multiple_choice.question: In "The Development of Values," which influence is described as shaping values partly through observation rather than explicit instruction alone?
options: A. institutions
B. parents
C. life events
D. peers
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. parents

multiple_choice.question: Which section most explicitly connects value formation to the balance between conformity and individuality?
options: A. peers
B. life events
C. institutions
D. religion and culture
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: D. religion and culture

multiple_choice.question: The example of a school taking a public stand against bullying is used to illustrate how
options: A. peers shame each other into obeying social norms.
B. institutions promote a code of ethics.
C. parents model moral behavior at home.
D. life events force people to reconsider priorities.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. institutions promote a code of ethics.

multiple_choice.question: Which scenario is presented as an ethical dilemma because it pits two positive values against each other?
options: A. wanting to gain fame and money
B. choosing between truthfulness and protecting a close friend
C. deciding whether to change jobs
D. deciding whether to disagree with a teacher
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. choosing between truthfulness and protecting a close friend

multiple_choice.question: In the classification exercise on page 21, why does "celebrities" not fit with "peers / acquaintances / colleagues"?
options: A. It refers mainly to people known through public awareness rather than everyday direct contact.
B. It is singular while the others are plural.
C. It describes a moral quality rather than a social category.
D. It applies only to childhood relationships.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: A. It refers mainly to people known through public awareness rather than everyday direct contact.

multiple_choice.question: Which quotation on page 21 most directly supports religion as a source of values?
options: A. "They taught me to work hard."
B. "Those are the guidelines that help me remember the difference between right and wrong."
C. "Children see everything."
D. "A sudden financial loss may force us to re-examine what is important to us."
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. "Those are the guidelines that help me remember the difference between right and wrong."

multiple_choice.question: Which definition in the philanthropy section depends least on wealth and most on effort toward social or political change?
options: A. donor
B. philanthropist
C. activist
D. humanitarian
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. activist

multiple_choice.question: Which distinction is most faithful to the textbook definitions?
options: A. A donor must be famous, whereas a philanthropist need not be.
B. A philanthropist gives only money, whereas a humanitarian gives only time.
C. A donor is any giver to a cause, whereas a philanthropist is described as wealthy and significant in scale.
D. An activist and a humanitarian are defined in exactly the same way.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. A donor is any giver to a cause, whereas a philanthropist is described as wealthy and significant in scale.

multiple_choice.question: Which biography centers on opposition to nuclear weapons and nuclear power?
options: A. Graca Machel
B. Li Ka-shing
C. Helen Caldicott
D. Bono
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. Helen Caldicott

multiple_choice.question: Which issue appears as a labeled concern in the page 22 collage?
options: A. reforming tax policy
B. improving literacy
C. urban traffic safety
D. food inflation
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. improving literacy

multiple_choice.question: On page 24, which sentence contains a restrictive clause about a thing rather than a person?
options: A. Lara, who works in my office, told me she broke the camera.
B. The friend whose phone I lost insisted I didn't need to replace it.
C. She replaced the camera lens that she had broken the day before.
D. My laptop, which was always crashing when I really needed it, finally died.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. She replaced the camera lens that she had broken the day before.

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence from page 24 is explicitly given as a non-restrictive clause about place?
options: A. The hotel in the town where we stayed last weekend offered to give us a refund.
B. The Alpes Hotel, where we always get a room in July, offered to give us a refund.
C. The person who borrowed my camera yesterday just told me she had broken it.
D. She replaced the camera lens that she had broken the day before.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. The Alpes Hotel, where we always get a room in July, offered to give us a refund.

multiple_choice.question: The punctuation warning on page 24 is meant to show that commas can change
options: A. tense and aspect.
B. whether a clause identifies the noun or merely comments on it.
C. the pronoun from singular to plural.
D. direct speech into reported speech.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. whether a clause identifies the noun or merely comments on it.

multiple_choice.question: What broader writing lesson is reinforced by the instruction to add three commas and delete three commas in the essay task?
options: A. Every adjective clause should be enclosed in commas.
B. Punctuation must reflect whether information is essential or additional.
C. Relative pronouns should normally be omitted in formal writing.
D. Non-restrictive clauses should always begin with that.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. Punctuation must reflect whether information is essential or additional.

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence in Exercise C on page 24 is punctuated incorrectly because the final clause comments on the whole idea rather than identifying "Megan"?
options: A. His grandparents are the ones who taught him about right and wrong.
B. My cousin who was always truthful about everything told my aunt she was wrong.
C. I told a lie that I have regretted for more than ten years.
D. We found out that Megan was going to join us which was great.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: D. We found out that Megan was going to join us which was great.

multiple_choice.question: In Review B, sentence 1 distinguishes the mistake itself from Matt's response to it by showing that he tries to
options: A. take responsibility.
B. admit making a mistake.
C. shift the blame.
D. tell the truth.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. shift the blame.

multiple_choice.question: In Review B, sentence 3 most sharply separates silent repair from open confession; Alice wanted to take responsibility, but she did not want to
options: A. make up an excuse.
B. shift the blame.
C. admit making a mistake.
D. tell the truth.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. admit making a mistake.

multiple_choice.question: Review B, sentence 5 implies that the speaker values tact over blunt honesty in a case involving
options: A. a report that was not prepared on time.
B. a grandmother's dinner that tasted terrible.
C. an umbrella left on a train.
D. a missing sales file.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. a grandmother's dinner that tasted terrible.

multiple_choice.question: Which blank in Review C can only be completed by "where" rather than "when," "which," or "who"?
options: A. the time ___ she asked me to help
B. situations in ___ you simply have to meet your deadlines
C. the office is a place ___ "Actions speak louder than words"
D. a report ___ was due the next day
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. the office is a place ___ "Actions speak louder than words"

multiple_choice.question: In Review C, which pair of blanks requires preposition-plus-relative-pronoun structures referring respectively to a person and to a topic?
Which survey item on page 14 most directly tests honesty in a situation where confession is unlikely to be forced by witnesses?
options: A. you were late for an appointment
B. you forgot a friend's birthday
C. you damaged someone's car while parking, but no one saw you
D. you were caught telling a lie
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. you damaged someone's car while parking, but no one saw you

multiple_choice.question: Which preview response option reassigns responsibility instead of inventing a justification or admitting fault?
options: A. admit making a mistake
B. shift the blame to someone or something else
C. keep a promise
D. tell the truth
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. shift the blame to someone or something else

multiple_choice.question: Which pair of expressions in the preview vocabulary box stands in the clearest direct opposition within the theme of responsibility?
options: A. keep a promise / tell the truth
B. shift the blame / admit making a mistake
C. make up an excuse / keep a promise
D. tell a lie / admit making a mistake
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. shift the blame / admit making a mistake

multiple_choice.question: Which statement best integrates the unit's moral content with its grammar focus?
options: A. Grammar is treated mainly as an isolated form exercise unrelated to meaning.
B. Vocabulary sections are kept completely separate from the communication goals.
C. Grammar is repeatedly used to express evaluation, consequence, explanation, and responsibility.
D. The unit avoids using grammar to discuss ethics or social behavior.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. Grammar is repeatedly used to express evaluation, consequence, explanation, and responsibility.

multiple_choice.question: Which page asks learners not only to discuss consequences of lying but also to sort situations into those in which lies should or should not be told?
options: A. page 15
B. page 17
C. page 21
D. page 23
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. page 17

multiple_choice.question: Which expression from the unit is best classified as a comment clause rather than an identifying adjective clause?
options: A. who borrowed my camera yesterday
B. that she had broken the day before
C. where we stayed last weekend
D. which was embarrassing
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: D. which was embarrassing

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence best preserves the unit's intended informal pattern?
options: A. The participants lied to many of the people with whom they interacted.
B. The participants lied to many of the people who they interacted with.
C. The participants lied to many of the people whom they interacted.
D. The participants lied to many of the people with who they interacted.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: B. The participants lied to many of the people who they interacted with.

multiple_choice.question: Which detail in the values reading most clearly shows that values are cumulative rather than single-sourced?
options: A. Parents correct children when they make mistakes.
B. Some companies promote mission statements.
C. Values come from a variety of sources and combine to shape later choices.
D. Peers influence how we categorize other people.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. Values come from a variety of sources and combine to shape later choices.

multiple_choice.question: Which sentence from the writing page would lose its intended meaning if the commas were removed?
options: A. Lara, who works in my office, told me she broke the camera that she had borrowed.
B. The person who borrowed my camera yesterday just told me she had broken it.
C. She replaced the camera lens that she had broken the day before.
D. The friend whose phone I lost insisted I didn't need to replace it.
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: A. Lara, who works in my office, told me she broke the camera that she had borrowed.

multiple_choice.question: Across the unit as a whole, which response pattern is most consistently treated as the preferable one after wrongdoing?
options: A. conceal the act, then quietly repair the result
B. explain the circumstances and wait for others to decide
C. acknowledge the wrongdoing, accept responsibility, and try to make amends
D. deny bad intent and focus on the consequences for yourself
multiple_choice.correct_answer_text: C. acknowledge the wrongdoing, accept responsibility, and try to make amends

gap_fill.question:
Jason admitted that the whole incident was his fault.
Jason admitted that he ______ the whole incident.

While Mr. Rogg was out of the room, Joey started mocking the drawing.
While Mr. Rogg was out of the room, Joey started ______ the drawing.

Jason said he was unable to stop himself once everyone started laughing.
Jason said he ______ once everyone started laughing.

Jason and Joey let the joke go too far.
Jason and Joey ______ and let the joke go too far.

Dad said Jason needed to accept responsibility because the situation had become uncontrolled.
Dad said Jason needed to accept responsibility for letting things ______.

Dad told Jason to admit honestly what he had done.
Dad told Jason to ______ what he had done.

Dad believed Jason ought to apologize to the girl.
Dad believed Jason ______ the girl.

Jane never takes responsibility when she does something wrong.
Jane refuses ______ when she does something wrong.

The researchers found that every participant told one or more lies each day.
The researchers found that there was no day on which a participant failed to tell ______ lie.

The article suggests that honesty is not always rewarded in everyday life.
The article suggests that people are not always rewarded ______.

Some lies are told to protect the feelings of the people with whom we communicate socially.
Some lies are told to protect the feelings of the people ______.

The article says that people in highly social professions have the greatest opportunity to lie.
According to the article, people ______ frequent social contact have the greatest opportunity to lie.

In formal English, the preposition comes before the relative pronoun when referring to people.
In formal English, "the people who they interacted with" becomes "the people ______ interacted."

In formal English, the preposition also comes before the relative pronoun when referring to things.
In formal English, "the question which most people don't give a truthful answer to" becomes "the question ______ most people don't give a truthful answer."

I lost my friend's phone, but she insisted that I did not have to replace it.
The friend ______ insisted I didn't need to replace it.

The hotel in the town where we spent last weekend offered us a refund.
The hotel in the town ______ last weekend offered us a refund.

The person told me yesterday that she had broken the camera after borrowing it.
The person ______ just told me she had broken it.

My laptop finally died, and it had always been unreliable at the worst possible times.
My laptop, ______, finally died.

He blamed Paul for causing the accident, and that was totally unjust.
He blamed Paul for causing the accident, ______.

Formal English allows the preposition to come before the relative pronoun in clauses about things.
In formal style, "the deal that Nora talked about" becomes "the deal ______."

She had been working on the report for an entire month before the deal fell through.
The report ______ for an entire month was needed for an important client.

The speaker said she felt terrible after breaking the tablet.
The speaker said she ______ breaking the tablet.

Tim said replacing the tablet was unnecessary because he had already planned to buy a new one.
Tim said replacing the tablet was ______ because he had already planned to buy a new one.

The reading stresses that values do not develop from a single influence.
The reading stresses that values originate from ______.

The text says children often learn values by watching how their parents behave toward each other.
The text says children often acquire values ______ their parents relate to each other.

A sudden financial loss may force us to reconsider what matters most to us.
A sudden financial loss may force us to rethink ______ to us.

In ethical dilemmas, people may have to choose one value instead of another.
In ethical dilemmas, people may have to choose ______ values.

Religion can make it easier to distinguish right from wrong because it offers clear rules for living.
Religion can make it easier to distinguish right from wrong by providing ______.

In some cultures, people attach more importance to social rules than to individual choice.
In some cultures, people attach more importance to ______ than to individual choice.

A philanthropist may support a cause not only with money, but also with time or reputation.
A philanthropist may make a ______.
